title: 4. Top Guns | Gene and Roger
format: Episode 4 of the 'Gene and Roger' series, on how the blockbuster '80s turned Siskel and Ebert from public-TV underdogs into million-dollar corporate assets. Covers the 'My Dinner with Andre' rave that filled theaters, the sequel-and-slasher glut, and the Disney deal that poached them.

segments.heading: When a rave could fill a theater

films.title: My Dinner with Andre
films.note: Louis Malle's 90-minute dinner conversation between Wallace Shawn and Andre Gregory. Gene and Roger's late-'81 rave sent it from empty houses to a year-long New York run; a 15-year-old Alonzo Duralde saw it repeatedly and bought the screenplay.

films.title: Give My Regards to Broad Street
films.note: Paul McCartney's fantasy-musical Hollywood breakthrough. Gene interviewed him and opened by saying he didn't like it; the film flopped that same weekend.

films.title: Crocodile Dundee II
films.note: Raftery's cautionary tale: a week's allowance blown on a bad sequel, back when word of mouth was all you had to go on.

films.title: Tampopo
films.note: One of the low-budget films the two kept championing.

films.title: El Norte
films.note: Another small film they pushed.

films.title: The Brother from Another Planet
films.note: John Sayles indie on their list of movies worth keeping alive.

segments.heading: The decade of the blockbuster
segments.note: budgets went up, so did the risk

films.title: Rocky
films.note: Cost under $1M, proof a smash didn't need a fortune behind it.

films.title: Rocky IV
films.note: Produced a decade later for 30 times the budget.

films.title: Top Gun
films.note: Nearly $200M in theaters plus home video, the payoff that seemed to justify the spending. Also the episode's namesake.

films.title: Dune
films.note: David Lynch's expensive flop.

films.title: Staying Alive
films.note: The disastrous sequel to Gene's beloved 'Saturday Night Fever'.

films.title: Saturday Night Fever
films.note: Gene's favorite; 'Staying Alive' undid it.

films.title: Jaws
films.note: Proved nine-figure grosses were possible and changed what studios chased.

films.title: Superman
films.note: Another '70s mega-hit that reset expectations.

films.title: Star Wars
films.note: Both critics loved it; the blockbuster template it set still did damage to the Hollywood they knew.

films.title: Return of the Jedi
films.note: Backdrop to Gene and Roger's viral 'Nightline' spat with New York critic John Simon over whether Star Wars was just for children.

films.title: Beverly Hills Cop
films.note: Ruled the culture the year ticket sales hit a record $4B.

films.title: Ghostbusters
films.note: Same record year, the kind of hit that pulled more viewers to the show too.

films.title: Friday the 13th: A New Beginning
films.note: The 'Part 5' in a single episode where Gene and Roger reviewed four sequels and dragged out Aroma the educated skunk.

films.title: Police Academy 2: Their First Assignment
films.note: Another of the four sequels reviewed that day.

films.title: Porky's II: The Next Day
films.note: Third of the four.

films.title: Missing in Action 2: The Beginning
films.note: The fourth sequel of that lousy day.

films.title: Halloween
films.note: The '70s classic they loved, the counterexample to the slashers they went after.

films.title: Friday the 13th
films.note: Target of their 'Extreme Violence Directed at Women' special.

films.title: When a Stranger Calls
films.note: Same broadcast, built around a girl home alone and a phone that won't stop ringing.

films.title: Beyond the Valley of the Dolls
films.note: Duralde's gotcha: Roger co-wrote it, so his slasher outrage rang a little hollow.

films.title: I Spit on Your Grave
films.note: The film Duralde won't hear them condemn, given Roger's own résumé.

segments.heading: The thumbs that broke the pattern

films.title: The Thing
films.note: Roger panned it as the most nauseating thing he'd seen on a screen; Gene, to Roger's visible shock, liked it.

films.title: Critters
films.note: A Gene thumbs-up that scrambles the 'Gene was hard on popcorn' story.

films.title: Real Genius
films.note: Another Gene endorsement.

films.title: Beat Street
films.note: Gene compared the breakdancing drama to 'Saturday Night Fever' and only wished more of the dancing were shot better.

films.title: Mean Streets
films.note: The kind of film they could expect to walk in on early in their careers.

films.title: Nashville
films.note: From the same era, held to the same standard.

films.title: Scenes from a Marriage
films.note: Bergman, more of what made the '80s a letdown by comparison.

films.title: Repo Man
films.note: Off Raftery's own all-time '80s list.

films.title: Working Girl
films.note: Made Gene and Roger's best-of lists. They were never snooty about smart popcorn.

films.title: Who Framed Roger Rabbit
films.note: Another of their best-of-the-year picks.

films.title: Hollywood Shuffle
films.note: Robert Townsend's satire spoofed the pair as the 'Sneakin' in the Movies' critics.

Ads: Netflix FIFA World Cup Launch Edition

Give My Regards to Broad Street.title: Give My Regards to Broad Street

Beyond the Valley of the Dolls.title: Beyond the Valley of the Dolls
